decisions.decision: Haiku-only classification — Sonnet escalation removed entirely
decisions.rationale: Logs showed ~91% escalation rate due to 0.8 confidence threshold. Haiku calibration returns 0.6-0.79 even on clear-cut emails.

decisions.decision: Conversation tracking system rules disabled (TO_REPLY, AWAITING_REPLY, FYI, ACTIONED)
decisions.rationale: These rules triggered aiDetermineThreadStatus on every person-email using modelType='default'=Sonnet. Observed $0.016/email vs $0.004/email target. Saved ~$0.013/person-email.

decisions.decision: Phase 4 daily digest: batch all emails in one Haiku call at 9am
decisions.rationale: Reviewed 50 emails live via Gmail MCP. Snippets-only approach ($0.25/month) sufficient — but classification code already fetches full body truncated at 500 chars (stringifyEmail), which is even better signal. Batch call wins over per-email: cheaper (one system prompt), higher quality (relative importance, grouping), coherent narrative output.

decisions.decision: Two new rule categories identified for Phase 4
decisions.rationale: Live email audit found gap: (1) transactional emails (receipts, account alerts, approvals, card shipped, mail arrived) — real but don't need inbox after read, label+archive same day. (2) vendor marketing (emails from real vendors selling vs. notifying) — archive like newsletters. Current rules handle newsletters/marketing well but miss these two.

next_action: Option A: Complete Phase 3 verification — send 8 test emails per 03-05-PLAN.md, run psql queries A-G, paste results. Option B: Plan Phase 4 daily digest with batch architecture now that design is clear.
context_notes: Session 2026-05-02: Live Gmail audit via MCP (50 emails, last 24h). Classification is working — newsletters/marketing going to right labels. Gap is transactional emails (receipts, appointments, approvals) being swept away when they should be label+archive. Also confirmed: classification code sends full email body truncated at 500 chars (not Gmail snippet) — this is good signal for the digest too. Phase 4 digest design: 9am cron, pull threads via Gmail API, single Haiku call with subject+sender+body[:500] for each email, one bullet per email with expanded detail on important ones. Estimated cost ~$0.25-$0.50/month.
